⚠ Compliance Deadline

State & local government WCAG 2.1 AA compliance required as of April 2026. Healthcare Section 504 deadline: May 11, 2026.

Am I compliant? →

Language Access for Government

Your constituents speak every language.
We make sure you can reach them all.

Translation, interpretation, Section 508 remediation, and AI automation for federal, state, and local government agencies. Active GSA Schedule and NASPO ValuePoint contracts. Title VI and EO 13166 compliant. Veteran-owned VOSB.

GSA+NASPOActive contracts
Government agency inquiry

Tell us about your agency and language access needs. We'll confirm your contract vehicle and respond within 2 hours.

GSA & NASPO contracts active · Same-day confirmation
Your legal obligations

Every government agency serving LEP individuals
has these obligations. We help you meet all of them.

📜

Title VI & EO 13166

Requires meaningful language access for LEP individuals in all federally assisted programs. Includes translation of vital documents and provision of qualified interpreter services. Our Language Access Plans (LAPs) document your compliance.

Title VI FAQ →
♿

Section 508 & ADA Title II

Federal agencies: Section 508 applies to all ICT. State & local governments: ADA Title II WCAG 2.1 AA deadline is April 2026. We provide full audits, PDF remediation, VPAT creation, and ongoing monitoring.

Section 508 Services →
🏛️

Procurement Vehicles

We hold active GSA Schedule and NASPO ValuePoint contracts. No new procurement required. Issue a task order against your existing contract vehicle and receive services within 24–72 hours.

Procurement FAQ →
By agency type

We understand what your specific agency needs.

🏛️

Benefits Agencies

Applications, determination letters, appeals, and hearing interpreters. LEP outreach materials and Title VI LAP documentation.

🚔

Law Enforcement & Courts

Court-certified interpretation, case document translation, Miranda rights in 300+ languages, and public safety communications.

🌆

City & County Agencies

Community engagement, multilingual public notices, town hall interpretation, and permit/licensing document translation.

🏗️

Transportation & Transit

Title VI LEP plans, multilingual rider communications, accessible digital signage, and community meeting interpretation.

🏘️

Housing Authorities

Lease agreements, tenant rights notices, maintenance communications, and fair housing documentation in 300+ languages.

⚡

Utilities & Public Works

Emergency communications, multilingual rate notices, public hearing interpretation, and environmental justice outreach.

🏥

Public Health

Health emergency communications, vaccination campaign materials, community health program translation, and multilingual digital content.

🗳️

Elections Offices

Voting rights materials, ballot translation per Section 203, voter registration forms, and poll worker interpretation support.

How to order

No new procurement. No committee approval.
Order today on your existing contract.

1

Confirm your contract vehicle

Tell us your agency type and state. We confirm whether you can order via GSA Schedule, NASPO ValuePoint PA, or another available vehicle — same day.

2

Issue a task order

Your contracting officer issues a task order against the confirmed vehicle. We provide all required contract line item numbers, NAICS codes, and documentation for your procurement file.

3

Services begin within 24 hours

Upon task order receipt, we assign your project team, confirm your services scope, and begin delivery. Ongoing orders are serviced same-day or within agreed turnaround windows.

Already have a GSA or NASPO contract? You're ready to order.

No competitive bid required. No new procurement. Just a task order against a vehicle your agency already uses.

Confirm My Contract Vehicle →Procurement FAQ
Client results

Government agencies that trust us
with their language access obligations.

★★★★★

"We issued our first task order on a Monday morning. By Wednesday, we had a project manager, a confirmed translator roster for our seven priority languages, and our first batch of documents in progress. Faster than any vendor transition I've managed."

B
Brian Chen
Language Access Coordinator, State Health Department
★★★★★

"We were cited in an OCR investigation for inadequate language access. Language Access Hub helped us develop a Language Access Plan, translate our vital documents backlog, and set up OPI service — all within 60 days. The resolution agreement was satisfied ahead of schedule."

P
Patricia Okonkwo
General Counsel, County Human Services
★★★★★

"The veteran-owned status mattered to our procurement team, but what mattered to our program staff was that the interpreters actually knew our subject matter. Our benefits interviewers stopped getting confused calls from LEP clients trying to re-explain what was discussed."

N
Nancy Kim
Program Director, State Benefits Agency
Download the Government Language Access Compliance Checklist

A regulation-by-regulation guide covering Title VI, EO 13166, Section 508, ADA Title II, and more — with a vendor evaluation checklist for procurement teams.
